channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: iMessage still lags behind its peers. 4 ways Apple should update it for iOS 19
item.description: Few apps are as inextricably linked to the iPhone as Apple’s Messages. Introduced with the original iPhone almost 18 years ago, the app (then called “Text”) has become the primary messenger for most iPhone users worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: How to free up space on your Mac
item.description: If you need to free up space on your Mac, consider deleting duplicate photos in your image library. Apple makes it easy to do, and the CyberGuy explains the process.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google introduces Gemini 2.5 reasoning models
item.description: Google has introduced version 2.5 of its Gemini AI model, which the company said offers a new level of performance by combining an enhanced base model with improved post-training.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk sells X to his own xAI for $33 billion in all-stock deal
item.description: Elon Musk has sold social media site X to his own xAI artificial intelligence company in a $33 billion all-stock deal, the billionaire announced on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Musk says xAI buys social media platform X for $45 billion
item.description: Elon Musk said Friday his artificial intelligence firm xAI has bought his social media platform X. "xAI and X’s futures are intertwined. Today, we officially take the step to combine the data, models, compute, distribution and talent," Musk wrote in a post on X.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Ubuntu namespace vulnerability should be addressed quickly: Expert
item.description: Linux admins who have enabled the unprivileged user namespace restriction in their recent Ubuntu environments should take action to close three new vulnerabilities that allow a threat actor to bypass the supposed protection.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Firefox patches zero-day security flaw days after Chrome fixes the same issue
item.description: A bug similar to one recently found in Google Chrome was also spotted in Firefox and subsequently fixed.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple, Meta face modest fines as EU seeks to avoid US spat
item.description: Apple Inc. and Meta Platforms Inc. are set to be hit by fines under the European Union's Big Tech competition rulebook toward the end of next week, with the iPhone maker facing the added threat of recurring penalties under the bloc's Digital Markets Act.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Convicted of bilking investors, Nikola founder and Trump donor gets a presidential pardon
item.description: The founder of an electric car start-up sentenced to prison for exaggerating the potential of his technology has been pardoned by President Donald Trump, potentially wiping out hundreds of millions of dollars in restitution that prosecutors were seeking for bamboozled investors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Elon Musk privately pressured Reddit CEO Steve Huffman on content moderation: report
item.description: X owner Elon Musk was privately messaging with Reddit CEO Steve Huffman while also putting public pressure on the social media company’s content moderation efforts, The Verge reported Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: What is 'classified' information? What are 'secure' communications? Here's a primer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration's use of a popular messaging app to discuss sensitive military plans — with a journalist on the text chain — is raising questions about security and the importance of safeguarding the nation's secrets.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: An AI pioneer is launching a blockchain-powered marketplace for open-source models and applications
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: 3 keys to maximizing data strategies
item.description: Business leaders know it’s crucial to use identity-driven customer data to make smart decisions. But often, they get stuck because they don’t have a unified view of their customers and prospects. That’s a recipe for poor business outcomes.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The ‘New York Times’’s copyright lawsuit against OpenAI can proceed, says a federal judge
item.description: A federal judge has ruled that The New York Times and other newspapers can proceed with a copyright lawsuit against OpenAI and Microsoft seeking to end the practice of using their stories to train artificial intelligence chatbots. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft abandons data center projects as OpenAI considers its own, hinting at a market shift
item.description: OpenAI is reportedly contemplating building its own data center as Microsoft backs off on its own infrastructure investments. OpenAI has privately discussed building and operating its first data center to house storage, which is essential for developing sophisticated AI models.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft lauds Hyperlight Wasm for WebAssembly workloads
item.description: Microsoft has unveiled Hyperlight Wasm, a virtual machine “micro-guest” that can run WebAssembly component workloads written in a multitude of languages including C and Python. Introduced March 26, Hyperlight Wasm serves as a Rust library crate.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: White House taps Musk to investigate Signal chat mishap
item.description: The White House has asked Elon Musk and his team at the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to help investigate how a journalist was included on a Signal chat with national security officials.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Cyprus to fund desalination plants at hotels to reduce impact of water shortages on tourism
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — Cyprus said Wednesday it plans to subsidize construction of private desalination plants at hotels to ensure the tourism-reliant island nation has enough fresh water too see it through busy summer seasons when the demands of millions of visitors put a severe strain on dwindling
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Critical RCE flaws put Kubernetes clusters at risk of takeover
item.description: The Kubernetes project has released patches for five vulnerabilities in a widely used popular component called the Ingress NGINX Controller that’s used to route external traffic to Kubernetes services. If exploited, the flaw could allow attackers to completely take over entire clusters.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: Ethernet roadmap: AI drives high-speed, efficient Ethernet networks
item.description: At next month’s Optical Fiber Communication Conference and Exhibition (OFC), many of the technologies that are driving current and future Ethernet development will be on full display by the Ethernet Alliance, which is set to unveil its tenth anniversary Ethernet roadmap.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: What you need to know about Go, Rust, and Zig
item.description: Every language has a life cycle. Sometimes it starts with a relatively narrow use case and escapes its container; sometimes it’s intended as a general-purpose language but finds a powerful niche instead.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Napster sold to tech commerce company for $207 million
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A brand that was notoriously connected to music piracy before reemerging as a subscription music service has been sold to Infinite Reality for $207 million.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Two new AI tools help illuminate GSA’s future state
item.description: GSA's acting Administrator Stephen Ehikian said that the agency is breaking down the data silos to allow systems to better communicate with one another. The post Two new AI tools help illuminate GSA’s future state first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: The nation’s statisticians worry about the state of federal data gathering
item.description: One of the government's activities is the gathering of reliable statistics to guide policy and the quality of national statistics has become a concern. The post The nation’s statisticians worry about the state of federal data gathering first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Waymo plans to bring its driverless taxis to Washington in 2026
item.description: Waymo on Tuesday added Washington to its pioneering robotaxi service's steadily expanding list of U.S. markets, although passengers will have to wait until next year until they can take a driverless ride around the nation's capital city.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Fauna to shut down FaunaDB service in May
item.description: Fauna, the provider of the NoSQL database FaunaDB, has said that it will shut down the service by the end of May due to the unavailability of capital required to support the database service and market it.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cosmonic uses WebAssembly to manage apps
item.description: Leveraging the WebAssembly binary instruction format, Cosmonic has launched wasmCloud-based Cosmonic Control, an enterprise control plane for managing distributed applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A European startup scrubs its attempt to launch an orbital rocket on its first test flight
item.description: OSLO, Norway (AP) — A private European aerospace company scrubbed its attempt on Monday to launch the first test flight of its orbital launch vehicle from Norway.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: World Backup Day is March 31
item.description: Are you ready? World Backup Day is March 31, a day that has been recognized every year since it was first set up in 2011.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Apple fixes Passwords app vulnerability enabling Wi-Fi attacks
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says Apple's Passwords app had a three-month phishing flaw from iOS 18 to 18.2 patch.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
